title: Burgers conservative update preserves constant states

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For a constant grid state, the Burgers conservative update returns the same constant at every index.
assumptions: scale and c are real numbers
i is a natural-number grid index
the grid state is constant with value c
informal.expected_conclusion: Constant states are preserved by the Burgers conservative update.

declarations.name: burgersConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def burgersConservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := conservativeUpdate scale u (burgersFlux u) i

declarations.name: burgersConservativeUpdate_const_state
declarations.statement: theorem burgersConservativeUpdate_const_state (scale c : Real) (i : Nat) : burgersConservativeUpdate scale (fun _ => c) i = c

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: For a constant grid state, the Burgers conservative update returns the same constant at every index.
tasks.target: theorem burgersConservativeUpdate_const_state (scale c : Real) (i : Nat) : burgersConservativeUpdate scale (fun _ => c) i = c

tasks.input: Given the Burgers scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding the update, flux difference, and Burgers flux, then using ring.
